name: Pine Tree Hall Zen Community
one_liner: A working Sōtō Zen monastery presented as a hand-bound monastic broadsheet — off-white, sumi black, warm stone — with a contemplative landing scroll, lineage chart, schedule and retreats, dharma audio archive, visitor etiquette guide, and dana page.
domain: Religious / spiritual institution — Zen Buddhist meditation center
site_goal: Present Pine Tree Hall as a working monastery with public access — readable schedule, calendar-led retreats, deep dharma audio archive, transparent dana, etiquette/visitor primer — wrapped in a quietly editorial archival feel.
motif: Hand-bound monastic broadsheet: hairline ledger rules, right-edge seasonal marker column, matte tonal photographs (SVG-illustrated), tall narrow serif headings.

typography.display: Bodoni 72 / Bodoni Moda / Didot / Hoefler Text fallback — tall narrow serif
typography.body: Hoefler Text / Cormorant Garamond / EB Garamond / Garamond / Georgia — old-style serif
typography.japanese: Hiragino Mincho ProN / Yu Mincho / Noto Serif JP
typography.features: old-style figures, italic kickers, hairline rules, tabular numerals for times

pages.title: Retreat application — one question per screen

interactions_implemented: Right-edge vertical seasonal marker (24 sekki) that resolves to the current sekki by JS using today's date; collapses to a small fixed tag on mobile.
Lineage page is a horizontal-scrolling genealogical chart with hairline branches; clicking a portrait node opens a sidebar biography card; Escape closes.

Audio archive is filterable by teacher, theme and year via the left rail; selecting a talk updates the right-column 'now playing' panel and the persistent slim player fixed at the bottom of the viewport.
Schedule page has a filter row (All / Open to public / Residential) that hides or reveals ledger rows; hovering or tapping a schedule row reveals a one-line note.
Dana page has three quiet giving forms with suggested bands and free-entry fields; selecting a band syncs to the free amount, typing clears the band.
Retreat application is a multi-step form on its own page, one question per screen, no progress bar; the dana page also lists capacity and dana band for each retreat.
required_text_present: A working monastery, open to visitors.
Please observe silence past the second gate.
mobile_behavior: Right-edge sekki column collapses to a small fixed tag in the upper right. Ledger rows stack to single-column blocks. Lineage tree retains horizontal scroll within its scroller but with reduced padding. The archive layout collapses to a single column with the now-playing block below the talk list. Player narrows and hides its bar. Multi-step form keeps one question per screen.
assets: All imagery rendered as inline SVG to evoke matte tonal photographs (zafu, han, oryoki, gate, genkan, zendo, refectory, garden) plus an inked map. No external downloads.
